name: Chocolate Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Place racks in the upper and lower thirds of the oven.
2. Toast almonds in a jelly-roll pan until golden, then let them cool completely. Coarsely chop the cooled nuts.
3. Reduce oven temperature to 300°F. Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper.
4. In a bowl, whisk together flour, cocoa, instant coffee, baking soda, and salt.
5. Add one quarter of the dry ingredients mix and chocolate chips to a food processor; process until very fine. Combine with remaining dry ingredients. Set aside.
6. Whisk eggs, egg whites, sugar, vanilla, and almond extract in another bowl until frothy. Stir in the dry ingredients and nuts. The mixture will be thick and sticky.
7. Spoon batter into three 12x2-inch strips on the prepared cookie sheet, spacing each strip at least 2-1/2 inches apart. Use a metal spatula to even the edges.
8. Bake for 45 minutes in the lower third of the oven. Let cool on the sheet for 10 minutes while leaving the oven on.
9. Transfer the cooled loaves onto cutting boards. Cut into 1/2-inch slices with a serrated knife. Arrange the cut sides down on the cookie sheet. Bake for an additional 12 minutes per side. Cool completely.
